description.para: These macros are used to set/get the clock mode in CSI2 Tx.
CCM - continuous clock mode. NCCM - non-continuous clock mode.
These handlers are used to determine the type of the interrupt handler being registered with the MIPI CSI2 Tx Subsystem.
Since the subsystem is tightly coupled with the CSI2 Tx Controller driver, the handlers from the sub core are promoted to the subsystem level so that the application can use them.

briefdescription.para: Less no of lines recvd.
More no of lines recvd.
This typedef defines the different errors codes for Line Count status for a Virtual Channel when Frame End Generation is enabled.
Callback type which acts as a wrapper on top of CSI Callback.

parameterdescription.para: is a callback reference passed in by the upper layer when setting the callback functions, and passed back to the upper layer when the callback is invoked.
is a bit mask indicating the cause of the event. For current core version, this parameter is "OR" of 0 or more XCSI2TXSS_ISR_*_MASK constants defined in xcsi2txss_hw.h.

briefdescription.para: This function queries the subsystem instance configuration to determine the included sub-cores.

detaileddescription.para: For each sub-core that is present in the design the sub-core driver instance is binded with the subsystem sub-core driver handle

parameterdescription.para: is a pointer to the Subsystem instance to be worked.

briefdescription.para: This function initializes the included sub-core to it's static configuration.

parameterdescription.para: is a pointer to the Subsystem instance to be worked.

listitem.para: XST_SUCCESS If CSI sub core is initialised successfully
XST_FAILURE If CSI sub core inititlization fails

memberdef.argsstring: (UINTPTR SsBaseAddr, UINTPTR SsHighAddr, u32 Offset, UINTPTR *BaseAddr)
memberdef.name: Csi2TxSs_ComputeSubCoreAbsAddr

briefdescription.para: This function computes the subcore absolute address on axi-lite interface Subsystem is mapped at an absolute address and all included sub-cores are at pre-defined offset from the subsystem base address.

detaileddescription.para:
To access the subcore register map from host CPU an absolute address is required. The subsystem is aligned to 4K address and has address range of max 8K (0x00000-0x2000). By default, CSI is at offset 0x0_0000, and DPHY is at offset 0x0_1000.
SsBaseAddr
is the base address of the the Subsystem instance
SsHighAddr
is the max address of the Subsystem instance
Offset
is the offset of the specified core
BaseAddr
is the computed absolute base address of the subcore
XST_SUCCESS if base address computation is successful and within subsystem address range
XST_FAILURE Otherwise
None

briefdescription.para: This function initializes the MIPI CSI subsystem and included sub-cores.

detaileddescription.para: This function must be called prior to using the subsystem. Initialization includes setting up the instance data for top level as well as all included sub-core therein, and ensuring the hardware is in a known stable state.

parameterdescription.para: is a pointer to the Subsystem instance to be worked on.
points to the configuration structure associated with the subsystem instance.
is the base address of the device. If address translation is being used, then this parameter must reflect the virtual base address. Otherwise, the physical address should be used.

listitem.para: XST_SUCCESS if initialization is successful
XST_FAILURE, otherwise

briefdescription.para: This function is used to configure the CSI lanes and interrupts that are to be handled by the application.

detaileddescription.para: Refer to XCsi2Tx.h for interrupt masks.

parameterdescription.para: is a pointer to the Subsystem instance to be worked on.
is no of active lanes to be configure. This value ranges between 1 and 4.
Indicates Mask for enable interrupts.

listitem.para:
XST_SUCCESS on successful configuration of parameters
XST_FAILURE otherwise

simplesect.para: When EnableActiveLanes is 0, then the ActiveLanes parameter passed should be equal to Max Lanes of design.

briefdescription.para: This function is used to activate the CSI Subsystem.

detaileddescription.para: Internally it activates the DPHY and CSI.

parameterdescription.para: is a pointer to the Subsystem instance to be worked on.
is used to denote whether to enable or disable the subsystem

listitem.para: XST_SUCCESS on successful operation
XST_FAILURE on failed operation

briefdescription.para: This function is used to reset the CSI Subsystem.

detaileddescription.para: Internally it resets the DPHY and CSI

parameterdescription.para: is a pointer to the Subsystem instance to be worked on.

listitem.para: XST_SUCCESS if all the sub core IP resets occur correctly
XST_FAILURE if reset fails for any sub core IP fails

briefdescription.para: This function reports list of cores included.

parameterdescription.para: is a pointer to the Subsystem instance to be worked on.

briefdescription.para: This function gets the short packets.

parameterdescription.para: is a pointer to the Subsystem instance to be worked on.

briefdescription.para: This function is to set the Line Synchronization packet Generation status.

parameterdescription.para: is a pointer to the CSI2 TX SS Instance to be worked on.
0 : DISABLE 1 : ENABLE

briefdescription.para: This function is to set Generic Short Packet Entries.

parameterdescription.para: is a pointer to the CSI2 TX SS Instance to be worked on.
GSP 32 bit Entry

briefdescription.para: This function is used to get the Pixel Mode.

parameterdescription.para: is a pointer to the CSI2TX SS Instance to be worked on.

simplesect.para: 0x0 - Single Pixel Mode 0x1 - Dual Pixel Mode 0x3 - Quad Pixel Mode

briefdescription.para: This function is used to get the number of lanes configured in the IP.

parameterdescription.para: is a pointer to the CSI2TX SS Instance to be worked on.

simplesect.para: Max number of lanes available in u32 format

briefdescription.para: This function is used to check if lanes are in ulps mode.

parameterdescription.para: is a pointer to the CSI2TX SS Instance to be worked on.

listitem.para: 1 - ulps mode enter
0 - ulps mode exit

briefdescription.para: This function is used to set lanes in ulps mode.

parameterdescription.para: is a pointer to the CSI2TX SS Instance to be worked on.

listitem.para: 1 - ulps mode enter
0 - ulps mode exit

briefdescription.para: This function is used to set the CSI2 Tx Subsystem Clock Mode as either Continuous (0) or Non-Continuous (1) mode.

parameterdescription.para: is a pointer to the Subsystem instance to be worked on.
for Continuous Mode (0) or Non-continuous Mode (1)

briefdescription.para: This function is used to get the CSI2 Tx Subsystem Clock Mode as either Continuous (0) or Non-Continuous (1) mode.

parameterdescription.para: is a pointer to the Subsystem instance to be worked on.

simplesect.para: 0 - Continuous Clock Mode 1 - Non-continuous Clock Mode

briefdescription.para: This function sets the Line Count for virtual Channel if Frame End Generation feature is enabled.

detaileddescription.para: This is to be called before starting the core.

parameterdescription.para: is a pointer to the Subsystem instance to be worked on.
is which Virtual channel to be configured for (0-3).
is valid line count for the Virtual channel.

listitem.para: XST_NO_FEATURE if Frame End generation is not enabled
XST_INVALID_PARAM if any param is invalid e.g. VC is always 0 to 3 and Line Count is 0 to 0xFFFF.
XST_FAILURE in case the core is already running.
XST_SUCCESS otherwise

briefdescription.para: This function gets the Line Count for virtual Channel if Frame End Generation feature is enabled.

parameterdescription.para: is a pointer to the Subsystem instance to be worked on.
is which Virtual channel to be configured for (0-3).
is pointer to variable to be filled with line count for the Virtual channel

listitem.para: XST_NO_FEATURE if Frame End generation is not enabled
XST_INVALID_PARAM if any param is invalid e.g. VC is always 0 to 3 and Line Count is 0 to 0xFFFF.
XST_SUCCESS otherwise

briefdescription.para: This function looks for the device configuration based on the unique device ID.

detaileddescription.para: The table XCsi2TxSs_ConfigTable[] contains the configuration information for each instance of the device in the system.

parameterdescription.para: is the unique device ID of the device being looked up

simplesect.para: A pointer to the configuration table entry corresponding to the given device ID, or NULL if no match is found

briefdescription.para: This function returns the Index number of config table using BaseAddress.

parameterdescription.para: pointer to the instance structure
address of the instance

simplesect.para: Index number of the config table

briefdescription.para: This function performs self test on MIPI CSI Tx Subsystem sub-cores.

parameterdescription.para: is a pointer to the XCsi2TxSs core instance.

listitem.para: XST_SUCCESS if self test passed.
XST_FAILURE if self test failed.

briefdescription.para: This function is the interrupt handler for the MIPI CSI2 Tx Subsystem.

detaileddescription.para: The application is responsible for connecting this function to the interrupt system. Application beyond this driver is also responsible for providing callbacks to handle interrupts and installing the callbacks using XCsi2TxSs_SetCallBack() during initialization phase.

parameterdescription.para: is a pointer to the XCsi2TxSs core instance that just interrupted.

briefdescription.para: This function is used to disable the interrupts in the CSI core.

parameterdescription.para: is a pointer to the Subsystem instance to be worked on.
Indicates Mask for enable interrupts.

briefdescription.para: This routine installs an asynchronous callback function for the given HandlerType:

para.preformatted: HandlerType Invoked by this driver when: ------------------------- ------------------------------- (XCSI2TXSS_HANDLER_WRG_LANE) IncorrectLaneCallBack (XCSI2TXSS_HANDLER_GSPFIFO_FULL) GSPFIFOCallBack (XCSI2TXSS_HANDLER_ULPS) DPhyUlpsCallBack (XCSI2TXSS_HANDLER_LINEBUF_FULL) LineBufferCallBack (XCSI2TXSS_HANDLER_WRG_DATATYPE) WrgDataTypeCallBack (XCSI2TXSS_HANDLER_UNDERRUN_PIXEL) UnderrunPixelCallBack (XCSI2TXSS_HANDLER_LCERRVC0 LineCountErrVC0 (XCSI2TXSS_HANDLER_LCERRVC1 LineCountErrVC1 (XCSI2TXSS_HANDLER_LCERRVC2 LineCountErrVC2 (XCSI2TXSS_HANDLER_LCERRVC3 LineCountErrVC3

parameterdescription.para: is the XCsi instance to operate on
is the type of call back to be registered.
is the pointer to a call back function which is called when a particular event occurs.
is a void pointer to data to be referenced to by the CallBackFunc

listitem.para: XST_SUCCESS when handler is installed.
XST_INVALID_PARAM when HandlerType is invalid.

simplesect.para: Invoking this function for a handler that already has been installed replaces it with the new handler.

briefdescription.para: This function reads a value from a MIPI CSI2 Tx Subsystem register.

detaileddescription.para: A 32 bit read is performed. If the component is implemented in a smaller width, only the least significant data is read from the register. The most significant data will be read as 0.

parameterdescription.para: is the base address of the XCsi2Tx core instance.
is the register offset of the register (defined at the top of this file).

briefdescription.para: This function writes a value to a MIPI CSI2 Tx Subsystem register.

detaileddescription.para: A 32 bit write is performed. If the component is implemented in a smaller width, only the least significant data is written.

parameterdescription.para: is the base address of the XCsi2Tx core instance.
is the register offset of the register (defined at the top of this file) to be written.
is the 32-bit value to write into the register.

briefdescription.para: This function is the main entry point for the interrupt example using the XCsi2TxSs driver.

detaileddescription.para: This function will set up the system with interrupts handlers.

parameterdescription.para: is the unique device ID of the MIPI CSI2Tx Subsystem core.

listitem.para: XST_FAILURE if the system setup failed.
XST_SUCCESS should never return since this function, if setup was successful, is blocking.

simplesect.para: If system setup was successful, this function is blocking in order to illustrate interrupt handling.

briefdescription.para: This function initialize required platform-specifc peripherals.

listitem.para: XST_SUCCESS if required peripherals are initialized and configured successfully.
XST_FAILURE, otherwise.

briefdescription.para: This function sets up the interrupt system so interrupts can occur for the MIPI CSI2Tx Subsystem core.

detaileddescription.para: The function is application-specific since the actual system may or may not have an interrupt controller. The MIPI CSI Subsystem core could be directly connected to a processor without an interrupt controller. The user should modify this function to fit the application.

parameterdescription.para: is a pointer to interrupt controller

listitem.para: XST_SUCCESS if interrupt setup was successful.
A specific error code defined in "xstatus.h" if an error occurs.

briefdescription.para: This function is called when Wrong number of lanes are configured by the MIPI CSI2Tx Subsystem core.

parameterdescription.para: is a pointer to the XCsi2TxSs instance.
of interrupt which caused this event

simplesect.para: Use the XCsi2TxSs_SetCallback driver function to set this function as the handler for wrong lane configuration event.

briefdescription.para: This function is called when Generic Short Packet FIFO is found full by the MIPI CSI2Tx Subsystem core.

parameterdescription.para: is a pointer to the XCsi2TxSs instance.
of interrupt which caused this event

simplesect.para: Use the XCsi2TxSs_SetCallback driver function to set this function as the handler for Packet level error event.

briefdescription.para: This function is called when there is a change in ULPS state of D-PHY Lanes.

parameterdescription.para: is a pointer to the XCsi2TxSs instance.
of interrupt which caused this event

simplesect.para: Use the XCsi2TxSs_SetCallback driver function to set this function as the handler for ULPS mode detection event.

briefdescription.para: This function is called when a Line buffer is full event received by MIPI CSI2Tx Subsystem core.

parameterdescription.para: is a pointer to the XCsi2TxSs instance.
of interrupt which caused this event

simplesect.para: Use the XCsi2TxSs_SetCallback driver function to set this function as the handler for Line buffer full event.

briefdescription.para: This function is called when a event generated by unsupported data type in GSP request to the MIPI CSI2Tx Subsystem core.

parameterdescription.para: is a pointer to the XCsi2TxSs instance.
of interrupt which caused this event

simplesect.para: Use the XCsi2TxSs_SetCallback driver function to set this function as the handler for Short Packet FIFO error event.

briefdescription.para: This function is called when during packet transmission, byte stream FIFO is found to be starves for pixel by the MIPI CSI2Tx Subsystem core.

parameterdescription.para: is a pointer to the XCsi2TxSs instance.
of interrupt which caused this event

simplesect.para: Use the XCsi2TxSs_SetCallback driver function to set this function as the handler for Byte stream FIFO starve event.

briefdescription.para: For Microblaze we use an assembly loop that is roughly the same regardless of optimization level, although caches and memory access time can make the delay vary.

detaileddescription.para: Just keep in mind that after resetting or updating the PHY modes, the PHY typically needs time to recover.

parameterdescription.para: of seconds to sleep

briefdescription.para: This is the main function for XCsi2TxSs interrupt example.

detaileddescription.para: This is the main function for XCsi2TxSs self test example.

If the Csi2TxSs_IntrExample function which sets up the system succeeds, this function will wait for the interrupts. Once a connection event or pulse is detected, Csi2TxSs will TX device capabilities and re-start the subsystem.

listitem.para: XST_FAILURE if the interrupt example was unsuccessful.
XST_SUCCESS if the interrupt example was successful.
XST_SUCCESS if the self test example passed.
XST_FAILURE if the self test example was unsuccessful.

briefdescription.para: This function is the main entry point for the self test example using the XCsi2TxSs driver.

detaileddescription.para: This function check whether or not its sub-core drivers self test functions are in working state.

parameterdescription.para: is the unique device ID of the MIPI CSI2 TX Subsystem core.

listitem.para: XST_FAILURE if any of MIPI CSI2 TX Subsystem sub-core self test failed.
XST_SUCCESS, if all of MIPI CSI2 TX Subsystem sub-core self test passed.

briefdescription.para: Prevent circular inclusions by using protection macros.
Prevent circular inclusions by using protection macros.
This is main code of Xilinx MIPI CSI Tx Subsystem device driver.

detaileddescription.para: This file contains a design example using the XCsi2TxSs driver.
This file contains a design example using XCsi2TxSs driver with interrupts.
This file contains the implementation of the MIPI CSI Rx Subsystem driver's static initialization functionality.
This file contains self test function for the MIPI CSI Rx Subsystem.
This is the interrupt handling part of the Xilinx MIPI CSI2 Tx Subsystem device driver.
This header file contains identifiers and register-level core functions (or macros) that can be used to access the Xilinx MIPI CSI2 Tx Subsystem core.
Please see xcsi2txss.h for more details of the driver.

para.preformatted:
Ver Who Date Changes
1.0 sss 07/14/16 Initial release vsa 15/12/17 Add support for Clock Mode 1.2 vsa 02/28/18 Add Frame End Generation feature

detaileddescription.para: For more information about the operation of this core see the hardware specification and documentation in the higher level driver xcsi2tx.h file.

para.preformatted:
Ver Who Date Changes
1.0 sss 07/14/16 Initial release 1.2 vsa 02/28/18 Add Frame End Generation feature

detaileddescription.para: The interrupt registration and handler are defined here. The callbacks are registered for events which are interrupts clubbed together on the basis of the CSI2 specification. Refer to CSI2 driver for the event groups.

para.preformatted:
Ver Who Date Changes
1.0 sss 08/03/16 Initial release 1.2 vsa 02/28/18 Add Frame End Generation feature

Ver Who Date Changes
1.0 sss 07/14/16 Initial release

Ver Who Date Changes
1.0 sss 07/21/16 Initial release

detaileddescription.para:
It notify which of the following interrupt is generated by a print message
Incorrect Lane Configuration
GSP FIFO Full

Ulps State
Line Buffer Full
Unsupported/Reserved Data type
Pixel Data underrun

This example requires an interrupt controller connected to the processor and the MIPI CSI2Tx Subsystem in the system. For this example to display output, the user need to implement initialization of the system (Csi2TxSs_PlatformInit) and after MIPI CSI2Tx subsystem start (XCsi2TxSs_Start) is complete, implement configuration of the video stream source in order to provide the MIPI CSI2Tx Subsystem HIP input. The functions Csi2TxSs_PlatformInit and Csi2TxSs_StreamSrc are declared and are left up to the user implement.

para.preformatted:
Ver Who Date Changes
1.0 sss 08/02/16 Initial release ms 01/23/17 Modified xil_printf statement in main function to ensure that "Successfully ran" and "Failed" strings are available in all examples. This is a fix for CR-965028. 1.6 ml 11/15/23 Fix compilation errors reported with -std=c2x compiler flag

detaileddescription.para: It performs a self test on the MIPI CSI2 Tx Subsystem that will test its sub-cores self test functions.

para.preformatted:
Ver Who Date Changes
1.0 sss 07/14/16 Initial release ms 01/23/17 Modified xil_printf statement in main function to ensure that "Successfully ran" and "Failed" strings are available in all examples. This is a fix for CR-965028.
